label: AI Security Specialist
description: Secures AI and machine learning systems covering LLM red teaming, prompt injection, AI governance and risk management, adversarial machine learning, and AI supply chain security.

paths.name: AI Red Teaming Track
paths.rationale: For security practitioners pivoting into AI offensive security — LLM red teaming, prompt injection, and AI system exploitation. A rapidly growing specialization with very few credentialed practitioners.
pros: OSAI is the most credible AI red teaming cert — OffSec brand + 100% practical
HTB COAE adds practical adversarial AI depth co-developed with Google
PAPA provides an accessible entry point before advanced certs
Extremely scarce specialization — high demand relative to supply
cons: Very new certification space — employer recognition still developing
Rapid evolution of AI means content may date quickly

knowledge: Basic understanding of LLMs and generative AI
API testing and web application security basics
prerequisites.experience: Prior security practitioner background strongly recommended. AI red teaming builds on offensive security foundations — start with OSCP or BSCP before specializing in AI security.

cert_sequence.notes: TCM Security's AI pentesting entry-level cert. 100% practical exam covering OWASP LLM Top 10, prompt injection, and AI application testing. At ~$249 including training it's the most accessible starting point for AI security. Establishes the foundational methodology before more advanced credentials.

HTB's offensive AI expert cert co-developed with Google. Covers advanced LLM attacks, prompt injection chaining, model attacks, and AI vulnerability exploitation. 100% practical. With PAPA providing foundational methodology, COAE builds advanced offensive AI capability. The Google collaboration adds technical credibility to the curriculum.

OffSec's AI red teaming cert — released March 31, 2026. 24-hour proctored engagement + report covering LLM prompt injection, jailbreaking, agentic AI attacks, and multi-agent system exploitation. The most credible AI security cert given OffSec's brand and 100% practical format. By completing PAPA and COAE first you arrive at OSAI with genuine AI attack methodology rather than approaching a novel exam format cold.

paths.name: AI Governance & Defense Track
paths.rationale: For security professionals focused on AI governance, risk management, and defensive AI security — building secure ML pipelines, implementing AI risk frameworks, and advising organizations on responsible AI deployment.
pros: GIAC GMLE adds ML security depth from the most recognized security cert vendor
IAPP AIGP is the recognized AI governance credential from the privacy profession
ISO 42001 adds the emerging AI management system standard
Governance + technical combination is scarce and high-value
cons: Very new certification space — all these certs are nascent
Employer recognition still developing across the board
Governance focus is less technically hands-on

prerequisites.experience: Security governance or data science background beneficial. This is a cross-disciplinary role — security + AI + governance knowledge is the combination.

cert_sequence.notes: IAPP's AI Governance Professional — the most established AI governance credential, from the same organization that runs CIPP/E and CIPM. Covers AI risk management, EU AI Act, algorithmic accountability, and responsible AI frameworks. IAPP's established privacy practitioner network gives AIGP broader reach than newer AI-specific certs.

GIAC Machine Learning Engineer — covers ML security from a technical perspective including adversarial ML, model security, data poisoning detection, and secure ML pipeline design. The GIAC brand adds credibility in security practitioner hiring contexts. Open-book exam — build indexed notes organized by ML attack category and defensive control.

ISO 42001 AI Management System Lead Implementer — the emerging international standard for AI governance systems. As organizations face regulatory pressure from the EU AI Act and other frameworks, ISO 42001 provides a structured management system approach analogous to ISO 27001 for information security. Early movers in ISO 42001 implementation expertise will have a significant advantage as compliance becomes mandatory.

Certified AI Security Professional — covers the intersection of traditional security controls and AI-specific risk. 100% practical exam. Adds hands-on AI security validation to complement the governance credentials. The combination of AIGP + GMLE + ISO 42001 + CAISP represents the most complete AI security and governance credential stack currently available.
